title: Basement retaining wall seepage after rain
problem: Water seepage observed at basement retaining wall construction joint after heavy rainfall.
resolution: Inspect construction joint, verify waterstop condition, inject PU grout into active seepage path, repair honeycombing, and monitor with moisture log for 72 hours.

title: Honeycombing after column formwork removal
problem: Honeycombing found on lower section of reinforced concrete column after formwork removal.
resolution: Stop covering works, notify QA/QC, remove loose concrete, assess depth and rebar exposure, repair with approved non-shrink repair mortar, and document engineer acceptance before proceeding.

title: Rebar congestion causing poor concrete flow
problem: Concrete flow was blocked around beam-column joint due to dense reinforcement and limited vibrator access.
resolution: Escalate to site engineer before pour continuation, review temporary pour sequence, use smaller poker vibrator where permitted, confirm concrete workability, and capture photos for structural review.

title: False ceiling clash with MEP services
problem: Ceiling installation blocked by duct and cable tray clash above corridor.
resolution: Open BIM coordination issue, mark location on latest drawing, compare approved ceiling level with installed MEP elevation, request coordinated shop drawing update, and avoid ad-hoc cutting without approval.

title: Crack near lift core wall after concrete pour
problem: Hairline crack observed near lift core wall one day after concrete pour.
resolution: Record crack width and location, check curing and temperature records, inspect for movement or water ingress, consult structural engineer for classification, and apply approved monitoring or repair method.
